title: pseudohydrosme buettneri
intro: Pseudohydrosme buettneri, also known as the African pennywort, is a tropical plant species native to West Africa that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties and health benefits. Pseudohydrosme buettneri has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, and has also been found to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is also available in various herbal supplements and capsules.
